behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` does not match raw body, and do not forward event.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test of HMAC comparison can miss handler wiring bugs (e.g., verifying parsed JSON instead of raw bytes). MOCK-HIDE: mocking request/body plumbing can hide real signature failures. REAL-INFRA is not required if tested with in-process HTTP + real middleware + queue adapter.
behaviors.test_description: Start the HTTP handler with real raw-body capture middleware and a test queue implementation that records writes. Send a webhook with a deliberately invalid signature over an in-process HTTP client. Assert 401 response and assert zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: in-memory mocks can pass even if durability/write ordering is wrong. Need real queue persistence boundary (DB/file) with handler wiring. REAL-INFRA beyond local process is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler wired to the real persistent queue backend (local test DB/file). Instrument queue write completion and HTTP response timing. Send a valid webhook and assert the queue write commit timestamp is <= response-sent timestamp; assert HTTP status is 200.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unit tests can miss async orchestration mistakes in handler/queue/worker wiring. No real external API or OS primitive is required; local async components are enough.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to a real queue and a worker stub that blocks for a controlled delay (e.g., 10s) after dequeue. Send a valid webhook and assert HTTP 200 is returned quickly (well before worker completion), while worker eventually finishes later.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even when earlier events are slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is a wall-clock latency SLO under concurrency and event-loop/process contention; in-process mocks often hide scheduler/socket/backpressure effects. MOCK-HIDE risk is high for timing behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the service in a real Node.js process, drive concurrent HTTP traffic with real sockets, and pre-load slow downstream work from earlier events. Measure end-to-end response latency for new valid webhook requests; assert each target response is <3s under the defined load profile.

behaviors.description: Do not enqueue an event whose ID was already received.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness usually depends on persistent state/constraints and handler-to-queue interaction. Unit tests can miss race/transactional issues. No external network required.
behaviors.test_description: Use real persistent queue storage with dedupe keying enabled. Send the same valid event ID twice (including a concurrent variant). Assert first request enqueues once and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and include `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE: a mocked queue-full signal can miss real mapping of queue errors to HTTP semantics and header formatting. Requires real handler + queue boundary; not necessarily full system infra.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue capacity to a small limit in test storage, fill it to capacity, then send another valid webhook. Assert HTTP 503 and presence/valid format of `Retry-After`; assert overflow event is not enqueued.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects events exceeding limit.
behaviors.justification: This is a storage/queue module invariant across modules (capacity config + persistence + enqueue path). Unit tests of counters can miss transactional/persistence defects. REAL-INFRA can remain local DB/fs.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with a strict capacity (e.g., N). Enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue one more and assert a deterministic capacity-exceeded error with no extra persisted record.
